description:
Once .22.18 has the golden set covering every dumpable fixture, retire the per-directive substring checks. The goldens subsume them: a checked-in expected.clif pins the entire output, strictly stronger than any 'this substring must/must-not appear' predicate.

Plan:
- For each fixture with expect_clif_contains / expect_clif_excludes entries:
  * Fold the *intent* of each directive into the README.md body as prose (the WHY is what survives — 'under fz-ul4.27.15 add1_s2 emits no ishl_imm because int literals are raw-by-default').
  * Delete the directive from the frontmatter.
- Delete parse_fn_substr() and the assertion loop from fixture_matrix.rs.
- Delete expect_clif_contains / expect_clif_excludes fields from Header.

Net reduction in machinery. The golden is the assertion; the README is the documentation.

Invariants:
- tests still green
- every retired directive's intent is captured in README prose (search-grep the diff: no expect_clif_* directive should vanish without a corresponding prose addition)
- golden_clif / golden_specs continue to catch regressions
title: fz-ul4.27.22.19: fixtures — retire expect_clif_{contains,excludes} directives
